homepage.text_excerpt: Homepage - Ros Altmann Ros Altmann Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release ROS ALTMANN Ros is a leading authority on later life issues, including pensions, social care and retirement policy. Numerous major awards have recognised her work to demystify finance and make pensions work better for people. She was the UK Pensions Minister from 2015 – 16 and is a member of the House of Lords where she sits as Baroness Altmann of Tottenham. Ros Altmann Ros Altmann Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release Toggle navigation Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release Ros Altmann Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release Homepage Homepage VISIT ROS'S BLOG FOR HER VERY LATEST COMMENTS Visit Ros's blog to read her latest thoughts and comments and much more at pensionsandsavings.com CLICK THIS LINK WELCOME Welcome to Ros Altmann's website Welcome to Ros Altmann's website. An award winning expert on all aspects of pensions and later life policy, from investment strategy to company pensions, state pension policy, retirement and social care. She is also famous as a champion for the rights of older people, having spearheaded a multi-year campaign to force Government to restore lost pensions to 140,000 victims of the company pensions scandal. READ HER PROFESSIONAL PROFILE Follow Ros On Twitter READ MORE SOCIAL CARE - CRISIS AND REFORM Ros has been the recipient of a number of prestigious media awards for her pensions work READ MORE PENSIONS VICTORY Ros forced the Government to pay 140,000 people whose pensions were confiscated by law, despite Government assurances of safety. Read the history of appeals to Parliamentary Ombudsman, High Court and Court of Appeal which the Government lost each time. READ MORE PENSION FUND ASSET ALLOCATION AND INSTITUTIONAL INVESTMENT Ros' views on pension fund investment approaches, Liability Driven Investing, use of alternative assets and trends in asset allocation READ MORE INVESTMENT TRUSTS Ros highlights the important potential for UK investment companies to channel pension and retail long term investment to boost UK growth. READ MORE PENSIONS POLICY AND REFORM - RETIREMENT, SAVINGS, ANNUITIES Ros works on many policy areas. Click on the headings below to view any of the areas you are interested in. Pensions Policy and Reform – Retirement, Savings and Annuities Pension reform - State and private pension policy Women's pensions Auto enrolment Occupational pensions crisis - Defined Benefit Schemes Public Sector pension reform Annuities reform Savings Policy Pensions Administration MONETARY AND FISCAL POLICY Ros comments on Economic and Monetary Policy - particularly the impact of QE, interest rates, inflation and fiscal policy READ MORE BREXIT See some of Ros' press comments on Brexit READ MORE CAMPAIGNS FOR FINANCIAL JUSTICE Ros often campaigns for justice when she believes a group of people have been wronged READ MORE Pension Scams Highlighting the dangers of pension scams READ MORE CORONA CRISIS Ros's comments on the Covid-19 pandemic READ MORE RETHINKING RETIREMENT Older Workers Champion - Later Life Working. READ MORE ESG AND SUSTAINABLE INVESTING Press links relating to ESG (Environmental, Social and Governance) and Sustainable Investing. READ MORE PRESS RELEASES Ros writes regular releases to inform the media of her views on important issues READ MORE SPEECHES AND PRESENTATIONS Ros is often asked to give keynote speeches or presentations to industry gatherings and corporate events. Here are some of the PowerPoint presentations she used READ MORE ROS ON WEB See some of Ros' interviews and blogs on topical issues READ MORE LATEST PUBLICATIONS Ros publishes regularly, see her latest published work here READ MORE AWARDS Ros has been the recipient of a number of prestigious media awards for her pensions work READ MORE Search for: Search Recent Posts Homeowners ripping tax-free equity from their property to give to family Government urged to rethink ‘irrational’ and ‘sinister’ treatment of RE

pages.title: Profile - Ros Altmann
headings: h3: Ros Altmann
h3: Ros Altmann
h3: Ros Altmann
pages.text_excerpt: Profile - Ros Altmann Ros Altmann Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release ROS ALTMANN Ros is a leading authority on later life issues, including pensions, social care and retirement policy. Numerous major awards have recognised her work to demystify finance and make pensions work better for people. She was the UK Pensions Minister from 2015 – 16 and is a member of the House of Lords where she sits as Baroness Altmann of Tottenham. Ros Altmann Ros Altmann Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release Toggle navigation Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release Ros Altmann Home Profile Latest Publications Press Release Profile Profile Baroness Ros Altmann is an award-winning expert on later life issues, including pensions, long-term investment, finance, retirement planning and elderly care policy. She is an academic economist by training and was an institutional asset manager, heading up international pension investing for Chase and Rothschilds in London, before starting her own consultancy advising financial firms, pension funds, corporates, Governments and policymakers on pensions, investment and later life policies. She was UK Pensions Minister and now sits in the House of Lords and is a Visiting Professor at the London School of Economics. Ros was awarded a CBE in 2014 for services to pensioners and pension provision after her long career in the industry and was Director-General of Saga Group, the over 50s specialists from 2010 to 2013, She became the UK Government’s Business Champion for Older Workers from 2014 to 2015 and was Minister of State for Pensions from 2015 to 2016. For over forty years, Ros has worked with Governments, corporates, trustee boards, regulators and industry bodies and is well-known for her thought leadership as an expert voice on all aspects of pensions and later life policy. She is an experienced pension fund trustee, Non-Executive Director, has chaired public and private corporates, academic institutions and charities, including chair of investment and audit committees. For example, she was chair of pension fintech firm pensionsync and chaired the Court Funds Office and Ministry of Justice Strategic Investment Board. She is a Board adviser to pension MasterTrust provider Cushon, Huntswood Group and social enterprise business SalaryFinance. She has a deep understanding of both provider and customer viewpoints and has championed the cause of older people on many issues. Ros is a frequent media commentator on pension issues, regularly appearing on TV and radio, including BBC Radio4 ‘Today’ programme, You and Yours, World at One, BBC News at Ten, BBC Newsnight, Sky, ITV, Channel 4 and many international stations. She has also starred in various Panorama and ‘Tonight’ programmes. Her articles are widely published in the The Times, Financial Times, Daily Mail, Daily Telegraph, The Independent, Guardian, The Economist, New Statesman and is a sought-after keynote speaker of international renown. She has won numerous industry awards and honours including Pensions Personality of the Year (twice), Industry Guru of the Year, Women in Public Life Award, The Times ‘Business Bigshot’, and one of the UKs top 50 Most Influential People in Pensions. She has been involved in many initiatives to highlight financial injustice and help ordinary people who face difficulties with financial issues. The most high profile was the successful multi-year ‘Pensiontheft’ campaign which Ros spearheaded – totally unpaid – to achieve compensation for 150,000 workers who lost their final salary company pensions after being falsely assured by the Government that their pensions were completely safe, This led to the establishment of the Financial Assistance Scheme and the Pension Protection Fund. She also campaigned for compensation for the hundreds of thousands of Equitable Life victims. She was successful in highlighting the injustices of the UK annuity market and exposing the consumer detriment suffered by people buying u

( link to pdf ) Open External article Read More OPINION: Government must see pension investment as game changer for growth OPINION: Government must see pension investment as game changer for growth December 9, 2025 Ros Altmann Ros wrote a comment piece for the Jewish News explaining that using pension assets to boost growth can be the ‘game-changer’ the Government desperately needs so more of the £80bn in tax relief helps Britain, rather than being free to boost all other countries and not our own? ( link to pdf ) Open External article Read More Ros Altmann: Restricting salary sacrifice is a lose-lose policy Ros Altmann: Restricting salary sacrifice is a lose-lose policy December 5, 2025 Ros Altmann Ros’ Opinion Column in Money Marketing explains the damage that capping salary sacrifice is likely to inflict on DC pensions adequacy in future. ( link to pdf ) Open External article Read More Salary sacrifice cut will hit workers’ pay and pensions – and raise employer costs, warns ROS ALTMANN Salary sacrifice cut will hit workers’ pay and pensions – and raise employer costs, warns ROS ALTMANN November 29, 2025 Ros Altmann Ros wrote a Comment article for ThisIsMoney Daily Mail website, warning that the Government’s plans to cap salary sacrifice for pension contributions will damage future DC pensions and add to employer costs – but does not affect public sector schemes. Open External article Read More IHT plans could ‘kill off’ modern pensions IHT plans could ‘kill off’ modern pensions November 10, 2025 Ros Altmann Ros wrote a Comment piece for FT Adviser, explaining the damage to future pensions which is likely to be caused by the draconian retrospective IHT raid on unused pensions. Open External article Read More Lobbyists make final push for gov’t rethink over IHT on pensions Lobbyists make final push for gov’t rethink over IHT on pensions November 5, 2025 Ros Altmann Ros is quoted in Corporate Adviser, alongside many other experts giving evidence to the House of Lords Finance Committee about the need to change the Government’s proposals to impose IHT on unused pensions. Open External article Read More Ten ways Rachel Reeves might shake up pensions in the Budget, by former pensions minister ROS ALTMANN Ten ways Rachel Reeves might shake up pensions in the Budget, by former pensions minister ROS ALTMANN October 19, 2025 Ros Altmann Ros wrote a Comment article for Daily Mail’s ThisIsMoney, outlining ten potential pension reforms the Budget might consider – and warning against problems some would cause, while suggesting two that could be very positive. Numerous major awards have recognised her work to demystify finance and make pensions work better for people. She was the UK Pensions Minister from 2015 – 16 and is a member of the House of Lords where she sits as Baroness Altmann of Tottenham. ( link to pdf ) Open External article Read More Retirement Income Masterclass hosted by Baroness Ros Altmann Retirement Income Masterclass hosted by Baroness Ros Altmann March 15, 2021 Ros Altmann Comments 0 Comment This is an AssetTV Retirement Income Masterclass chaired by Ros with experts Kirsty Anderson from Prudential, Andrew Tully from Canada Life and Peter Glancy from Scottish Widows. Discussing the March 2021 Budget, trends to ESG, impact of interest rates and pension tax relief reforms. ( link to pdf ) Open External article Read More Pension funds should allocate towards infrastructure and growth assets as government bonds now provide “return-free risk”, says Baroness Altmann Pension funds should allocate towards infrastructure and growth assets as government bonds now provide “return-free risk”, says Baroness Altmann September 21, 2020 Ros Altmann Comments 0 Comment Institutional Asset Manager published extracts from Ros’ speech to the G7 Global Pensions Forum, calling for more pension fund investment in infrastructure or growth assets as Government bonds seem more like return free risks than risk free returns. ( link to pdf ) Open External article Read More Next Steps for Social Care Next Steps for Social Care September 16, 2020 Ros Altmann Comments 0 Comment Ros gave a presentation on the Social Care crisis to the Westminster Health Forum and explained the need for urgent reforms to ensure the sustainability of the current system. Open External article Read More First Actuarial Conference 2019 – Interview with Ros Altmann First Actuarial Conference 2019 – Interview with Ros Altmann May 1, 2019 Ros Altmann Comments 0 Comment Ros explains some of the many campaign issues she has worked on over the past 20 years and how she got into pensions originally – her determination to try to improve pension policy for people. Open External article Read More Presentation on Quantitative Easing Presentation on Quantitative Easing March 25, 2019 Ros Altmann Comments 0 Comment Slides from Ros’ presentation to the Royal Society on ‘QE – pension impacts and the end-game’ – the fallout from unconventional monetary policy has caused damage which is being ignored because it benefits such powerful groups. What will happen next? Open External article Read More Speech on Pension Liabilities Speech on Pension Liabilities March 25, 2019 Ros Altmann Comments 0 Comment Ros gave a speech at a Royal Society conference on pension liabilities, explaining the damage of QE to capital markets and pensions and commenting on how it might end.
